title: Strata — an optimizing compiler to WebAssembly
description: A from-scratch optimizing compiler that turns a small C-like language into real WebAssembly bytecode in your browser: SSA, SCCP/GVN/LICM/DCE/inlining, if-conversion to branchless select, and a relooper backend. Does floating point from scratch, both directions: str(float) is a genuine Dragon4 shortest-round-trip formatter written in the language itself, and parse_float its correctly-rounded inverse. Now ships a full transcendental math library (exp/ln/log2/log10/sin/cos/tan/asin/acos/atan/atan2/sinh/cosh/tanh/pow/cbrt/hypot/fmod, plus sqrt/floor/round/…) written ONCE as a shared Strata kernel that the wasm backend compiles and the reference interpreter runs — so they agree bit-for-bit, and a dedicated oracle proves each is within ~1 ULP of libm. Plus a real f32 single-precision type lowered end to end to the wasm f32 opcodes (arithmetic, compare, conversions, f32[], f32 struct fields), structs with nested/recursive layouts and a null handle, 64-bit long → wasm i64 (BigInt-exact, hex/L literals, long[], str(long)), bit primitives (popcount/clz/ctz/rotl/rotr), do-while/switch, a string standard library with str[] (split/join), a step debugger, and a differential test suite proving the wasm matches a reference interpreter at every opt level. NEW: first-class functions — a fn(…)->R type whose values are real function pointers, called through a wasm function table and call_indirect, usable in variables/params/returns/struct-field vtables, with a devirtualization pass that turns provable indirect calls back into direct ones at -O1+; this unlocks genuine higher-order programming (map/reduce, a comparator sort, compose). NEWEST: arrays of function pointers — (fn(…)->…)[] via fn_array(n) — a real jump table / state machine, with function-table slot 0 reserved as a null funcref so an unset slot traps on call and fn == null is an observable 'is this wired up?' test; the flagship is a stack-based bytecode VM whose entire decode step is one table[op](vm, imm) lowering to call_indirect. All proven by the differential harness (636 checks across -O0..-O3).
